title: Docker Networking
description: Learn to create networks, connect containers, inspect network topology, and publish ports.

tasks.prompt: List all Docker networks on the host.
tasks.explanation: docker network ls shows bridge, host, and custom user-defined networks.
User-defined bridge networks provide DNS-based container discovery.

tasks.prompt: Run 'nginx' container 'web' attached to network 'app-net' in detached mode.
tasks.explanation: --network attaches the container to a specific Docker network at start.

tasks.hint: Use docker network connect app-net api.
tasks.explanation: docker network connect adds a running container to another network.

tasks.hint: Use docker network disconnect app-net api.
tasks.explanation: disconnect removes a container interface from the specified network.

tasks.prompt: Inspect network 'app-net' to view connected containers.
tasks.explanation: Inspect shows subnets, gateways, and attached endpoints.

tasks.prompt: Run 'nginx' mapping host port 8080 to container port 80 in detached mode.
tasks.explanation: -p publishes container ports to the host for external access.

tasks.prompt: Run 'redis' binding port 6379 only to localhost (127.0.0.1).
tasks.explanation: Binding to 127.0.0.1 restricts access to the local machine.
Networks with active endpoints cannot be removed until disconnected.

tasks.prompt: Run 'nginx' using the host network stack.
tasks.explanation: host networking removes network isolation and uses the host interfaces directly.
